activity_question: Where was Africa's first climate summit held?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for Africa to have its own climate summit?

title: Mozart's Dramatic Opera Takes the Stage
story: Thunderous applause filled the Estates Theatre in Prague as the curtain rose. Wolfgang Mozart's new opera, 'La Clemenza di Tito', captivated the audience with its powerful music and gripping story. This exciting premiere on September 6, 1791, showcased Mozart's incredible talent for blending drama and melody.
activity_question: Where did Mozart's opera 'La Clemenza di Tito' premiere?
follow_up_question: What kind of music is an opera?

title: Boxing Makes Radio History
story: Pow! Bam! The crowd roared as Jack Dempsey's fists flew. But this fight was special. In 1920, on September 6, boxing fans experienced something new. For the first time ever, people could listen to a prizefight on the radio! Dempsey knocked out Billy Miske in round three, defending his heavyweight title. This broadcast changed sports forever.
activity_question: What made this boxing match special?
activity_choices: It was the first radio broadcast of a prizefight
It was the longest fight in history
activity_answer: It was the first radio broadcast of a prizefight
follow_up_question: How do you think radio changed the way people enjoyed sports?

title: A Bittersweet Steamboat Adventure
story: Laughter mixed with tears as 'Steamboat Round the Bend' hit theaters in 1935. This comedy film, directed by John Ford, starred the beloved Will Rogers. Sadly, Rogers had died in a plane crash just weeks before the movie's September 6 release. His final performance became a touching farewell to fans.
activity_question: Who directed the film 'Steamboat Round the Bend'?
follow_up_question: How did Will Rogers pass away before the film's release?

title: Elvis Rocks the Christmas Spirit
story: Jingle bells met rock 'n' roll when Elvis Presley hit the recording studio in 1957. The King of Rock belted out holiday classics like 'White Christmas,' 'Silent Night,' and 'Here Comes Santa Claus' on September 6. Elvis added his signature swagger to these timeless tunes, creating a unique blend of traditional cheer and hip-shaking cool.
activity_question: Which Christmas songs did Elvis record?
activity_choices: 'White Christmas,' 'Silent Night,' and 'Here Comes Santa Claus'
'Blue Christmas,' 'Santa Claus Is Coming to Town,' and 'Let It Snow'
'Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree,' 'Little Drummer Boy,' and 'Feliz Navidad'
activity_answer: 'White Christmas,' 'Silent Night,' and 'Here Comes Santa Claus'
bonus_fact: Elvis's Christmas album became one of the best-selling holiday records of all time.
